conversation.content:
Here is my data and morning briefing:
# 🌅 Morning Briefing — Wednesday, May 6, 2026
**Gina Mancuso | 32 Days to Hyrox Open**

---

## 1. Recovery Status
Readiness at 85 with balanced HRV and a strong Body Battery recharge of 71 — you're in a good window today. Left hip is still early in its healing arc (7 weeks post-op), so this isn't a green light to push hard, it's a green light to train *smart*.

---

## 2. What the Data Says

- **Readiness trend is rebounding.** You dipped into the 73–76 range mid-week last week, and you're back at 85 today — that recovery curve is responding well to whatever you did (or didn't do) yesterday.
- **Training volume has been low.** One logged session in the last 7 days. With 32 days to the Open, that gap matters. The good news: your body is clearly recovering. Now it's time to start building again — carefully.
- **Yesterday's calories were under by ~436.** You're 7 weeks post-op on the left hip and in active PT. Under-fueling at this stage slows tissue remodeling. Protein was close (128/143g), but total energy was short.

---

## 3. Today's Training Recommendation

You've already logged **Indoor Cycling** — good start. Here's how to structure the rest of the day:

**Morning Ride (if not yet complete):**
- 30–40 min | Zone 2 aerobic effort (RPE 5–6 / ~65–75% max HR)
- Flat resistance, smooth cadence — no heavy standing climbs that load the left hip

**Afternoon / After School — Strength (30–35 min):**
Focus: Right quad activation + bilateral hip stability
- Seated leg press (bilateral, moderate load) — 3×12
- Terminal knee extensions (band) — 3×15 each side
- Side-lying clamshells — 3×15 (left side controlled, no pain)
- Single-leg Romanian deadlift (right leg emphasis, use support if needed) — 3×8
- Dead bug — 3×10 (core without hip flexor loading)

> ⚠️ Left hip: No single-leg loading on the left yet unless cleared by PT. Right quad is your priority weakness heading into Hyrox — own it today.

---

## 4. Nutrition Advice

Yesterday's under-eating (78% of goal) is the one thing to correct today — especially with training logged.

- **Hit your 1,913 calories today. Non-negotiable on a training day.**
- Add one substantial carb source post-ride (banana + rice cakes, oatmeal, or a wrap) — you had plenty of fat and decent protein, but carbs fuel your sessions and spare muscle.
- **Pre-strength snack:** ~20–25g protein + simple carb 60–90 min before (Greek yogurt + fruit or a protein shake + banana)
- **Post-strength:** Full meal with 35–40g protein within 60 min — this is your tissue repair window at 7 weeks post-op.
- Aim for **145g+ protein today** to close yesterday's gap.

---

## 5. One Thing to Watch

**Left hip fatigue signals during cycling.** If you feel anterior hip pinching, clicking, or the left side starting to compensate in your pedal stroke — back off resistance immediately. At 7 weeks, the joint is stronger than it was, but it's not ready to absorb load disguised as "just cardio."

---

## 6. Coach's Note

You've had two hip surgeries in five months while teaching full-time, parenting solo, and grinding through a graduate degree. And you're still targeting a Hyrox Open in 32 days. That's not delusion — that's discipline with a deadline. But the data is clear: you can't outcompete a calorie deficit and a training gap at the same time. This week is about stacking consistent, quality days — not making up for lost time in one session. The version of you that races in June is being built *right now*, in the decisions you make between 6am and bedtime. Eat
I've reviewed your data and generated your briefing. What questions do you have?
